rules.text: Confidence without arrogance - be sure of yourself without putting the other person down.
Respect boundaries. If someone gives short answers, changes the subject, or steps back physically, that's your answer.
Read interest levels honestly. Don't talk yourself into signals that aren't there.
Don't pressure people for a number, a plan, or a response. Ask once, respectfully, and let it be their call.
Accept rejection gracefully. A calm 'no worries, have a good one' costs you nothing and shows more character than pushing.
Be authentic. A line that isn't you will fall flat the moment a real conversation starts.
Treat people as individuals, not as a category or checklist - archetypes and context here are conversation starters, not conclusions.
Any escalation - physical or otherwise - should follow clear mutual enthusiasm, not just the absence of a 'no'.
